arXiv.title: Floquet MÃ¶bius topological insulators
arXiv.abstract: MÃ¶bius topological insulators have dispersive edge bands with MÃ¶bius twists in momentum space, which are protected by the combination of chiral and $Z_2$-projective translational symmetries. In this work, we reveal a unique type of MÃ¶bius topological insulator, whose edge bands could twist around the quasienergy $Ï$ of a periodically driven system and are thus of Floquet origin. By applying time-periodic quenches to an experimentally realized MÃ¶bius insulator model, we obtain interconnected MÃ¶bius edge bands around zero and $Ï$ quasienergies, which can coexist with a gapped or gapless bulk. These MÃ¶bius bands are topologically characterized by a pair of generalized winding numbers, which are integer-quantized due to an emergent chiral symmetry at a high-symmetry point in momentum space. Numerical investigations of the quasienergy and entanglement spectra provide consistent evidence for the presence of such MÃ¶bius topological phases. A protocol based on the adiabatic switching of edge-band populations is further introduced to dynamically characterize the topology of Floquet MÃ¶bius edge bands. Our findings thus extend the scope of MÃ¶bius topological phases to nonequilibrium settings and unveil a unique class of MÃ¶bius-twisted topological edge states without static counterparts.
